summary_en: ROE target 8%+ (vs. current 5.9% five-year average and 8% cost of capital). PBR sustained below 1.0x for five consecutive periods. Improvement measures: geographic expansion (Kansai base strengthening, Chubu/Kanto footprint expansion), dividend policy emphasis (payout ratio now 21.3% vs. prior 17%), shareholder privilege program launched FY3/26. Continuous ROE advancement through capital-cost-conscious management framework.
title_en: Measures for Realization of Management Conscious of Capital Costs and Share Price
translation_en_brief: Harima-Kyowa filed a capital cost and shareholder value improvement framework on 14 May 2026. The company's five-year average ROE of 5.9% trails its estimated cost of equity (8%), and its price-to-book ratio has remained below 1.0x for five consecutive periods. In response, the board adopted a dual strategy: sustained organic growth through geographic expansion (strengthening Kansai distribution and logistics operations while building capacity in Chubu and Kanto) and enhanced shareholder returns. The payout ratio has risen from mid-teens to 21.3% in FY3/26, and a new shareholder privilege scheme was introduced in the same period. The company commits to continuous ROE improvement and capital-cost-conscious management practices going forward.
